observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer those completed projects from the CIP accounts to their appropriate Infrastructure Asset accounts and provide the related depreciation expense. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant constantly coordinate with the Municipal Engineer regarding the status of the projects in the CIP accounts as at the end of the reporting period to determine which of those are already complete and to make the necessary reclassification thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to revisit the PPE schedule as of December 31, 2021 and immediately make a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust and reclassify various PPE items to its proper accounts and submit to the audit team for audit evaluation.  We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant henceforth capitalize expenditure items to PPE pursuant to the provisions of Item 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately trace the cause of the discrepancy of ₱71,577.90 and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of Loans Payable-Domestic as of December 31, 2021.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to review the reconciling items, make the necessary adjustments and seriously work on these to clear the discrepancy. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant to update the preparation of BRS of all current and savings accounts to ensure monthly reconciliation of the Cash in Bank so that reconciling item, if any, shall be corrected/adjusted immediately to avoid accumulation. Lastly, we recommended that Management prepare the BRS per savings or current accounts maintained instead of a consolidated BRS to facilitate identification of reconciling items.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to trace the differences of the Cash Local Treasury and Cash in Bank-LCCA accounts. We further recommended that Management henceforth, conduct properly the monthly reconciliation between the treasury records as against accounting records in compliance with Section 63 (F) of the LTOM.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor submit to the Audit Team an authority from the Local Sanggunian for opening and maintaining savings accounts of the Municipality with the Development Bank and Land Bank, including that from ASPAC, otherwise instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately close these accounts and transfer the funds to the Municipality’s main depository accounts.

We further recommended that those current accounts set up with specific purpose, such as for the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) project and those for the Rural Health Unit (RHU) personnel, immediately implement the programs/projects/ activities in which such accounts were set-up in order to fully utilize the available resources and to avoid these accounts of becoming dormant. Lastly, we recommended that Management revisit the purpose of opening these current and savings accounts and take appropriate actions in accordance with the above-stated provisions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately initiate steps in the designation of an Officer-in-Charge for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for approval by the BLGF, and henceforth, comply with Section 12 of the LTOM whenever temporary or permanent vacancy of such office occurs.

We recommended that Management assess the manpower needs of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and initiate steps towards compliance with DBM LBC No. 137 pertaining to position titles in local government units.  We further recommended that Management immediately stop the practice of designating Job-Order personnel as collecting officers of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to religiously and diligently comply the provisions of Section 63 of the LTOM.  We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor make a periodic monitoring on the compliance of the above-stated provisions.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the monthly BRS and immediately submit to the Audit Team for verification pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011.  We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant henceforth prepare the bank reconciliation statement separately for each of the existing depository accounts of the Municipality in order to facilitate reconciliation between the book and bank balances of all accounts as at a given period.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal General Services Officer to initiate the disposal of the unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of P.D. 1445, Section 379 of R.A. 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular 89-296.

We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the registration; transfer and titling of these parcels of land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in compliance with Section 39 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Officer to explain as to why these medical equipment were yet to be used in its full capacity despite the lapse of almost one year. We further recommended that the Municipal Health Officer observe faithfully the functions and duties embodied in R.A. No. 7160 so that the procured goods, equipment and services would be dealt in the most economic, efficient and effective manner.

We recommended that Management immediately implement the priority projects in order to provide the intended benefits due to its constituents and facilitate liquidation thereof. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant initiate the return of the unused funds to the source agencies so as to clear the books of the said liability and present a reliable balance of the account.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the documentary requirements needed on these infrastructure projects undertaken by administration for project inspection. Henceforth, submit copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all its supporting documents within five working days from execution/issuance pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management review the implementation of this project and compute for liquidated damages, if necessary.  We also recommended that Management effectively monitor the timelines on the completion of projects so as to provide the needed basic services to its constituents in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the LDRRM Officer incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRM fund of the previous years, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and the related subsequent issuances particularly on the propriety of charging of expenditures to the LDRRMF.  We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to diligently check the purpose of disbursements to ensure correctness in charging of expenses in relation to funding source.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the pertinent provisions stated in P.D. 1445, the 2016 RIRR of R.A. 9184 and DBM NBC No. 563 to avoid the incurrence of unconscionable or excessive expenditures which are disallowable in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the BAC to submit the documentary requirements of these contracted suppliers in order to regularize these procurement transactions. Henceforth, we recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions stated in the 2016 RIRR of R.A. 9184 concerning the guidelines on the procurement of goods & services through Alternative Mode of Procurement particularly under Negotiated Procurement-SVP.

We recommended that the Management procure the common-use supplies and equipment from the Cebu Regional Depot of PS-DBM in accordance with DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011, Administrative Order No. 17 and pertinent provisions of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.   We also recommended that Management comply with the documentary requirements stated in GPPB Resolution No. 17-2020 for those CSE items which are not available in the PS-DBM at the time of procurement before pursuing the procurement from other sources.

Municipal Mayor direct the GSO prepare separate property cards on all classes of PPE in the form as prescribed by the GAM for LGUs.  We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate ledger cards of all PPE accounts.  Lastly, we recommended management the immediate submission of the Report on Physical Count of all government-owned properties of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Assess its existing organizational structure so that a reasonable number of plantilla personnel will be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job-order personnel. observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 | The Human Resource Management (HRM) Office of the LGU facilitated a workshop on the formulation of a staffing plan for the LGU at the Bai Hotel which was co-facilitated by the CSC Cebu North Field Office Director, Merlinda F. Quillano.  The aim is to come up with a localized staffing plan based on necessity and urgency of demand in every office/department because the LGU recognizes the importance of an organized pattern or structure to ensure continuous turnover of positions either by promotion or appointment of new applicants.  The said Staffing Plan is also part of the LGU’s components in the Prime HRM program of the LGU that is closely monitored and evaluated by the CSC.  The HRM Office intends to finalize the said plan within the year and to forward the same to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office is already utilizing the eNGAS accounting system.  The difference was brought by a system limitation wherein it does not accept beginning balances in negative amounts.  The Trust Fund (TF) has a negative balance due to delayed transfer of the equity amount from General Fund (GF) of DILG project, and erroneous charging of the Budget Officer to TF instead of GF.  Consequently, Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) had been made to correct the balances of these respective accounts.

observations.recommendation: Inform us of subsequent developments pertaining to the actions taken on its conduct of general revision of property assessment in conformance with Section 219 of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
For reason of being a part of the provincial government, it is the Office of the Provincial Assessor that shall perform such duties and functions as may be essential to the administration of the real property tax.

Starting last January 20, 2020, this office had just started the conduct of general revision of 2020 pursuant to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan of Cebu Ordinance No. 2019-17 and Office of the Provincial Assessor Memorandum Circular No. 01-2020, which will take effect on July 1, 2020.  Moreover, we have submitted to the Municipal Treasurer copies of assessment roll, not just on the 31st of December but for every quarter of each year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management immediately implement the projects / programs / activities funded by the National Government and other agencies in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the implementation of those PPAs.

Also, the Municipal Accountant should be directed to initiate timely liquidation and return the unexpended balances to the source agency after the completion of the intended projects/programs/activities in compliance of the aforecited regulation.

We recommend that the management immediately implement the priority projects in order not to deprive the constituents of the benefits due them and facilitate liquidation thereof.  Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant exert efforts in identifying and documenting the details of the fund balances to facilitate settlement by the barangay with the source agency so as to fairly present the balance of the account in the financial statements.

We recommend that management create a task force to identify the lots owned by the Municipality and review if the same are titled.  Moreover, require the task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots.  Likewise, we require the Municipal Accountant to update/adjust the Land account balance in the books based on the results of the identification of municipal-owned lots and its titling.

We recommend management to review the reconciling items, make the necessary adjustments and seriously work on these to clear this account of any discrepancy.   We further advise the Municipal Accountant to update the preparation of BRS of all current and savings accounts to ensure monthly reconciliation of the Cash in Bank and ensure that any reconciling item shall be corrected / adjusted immediately to avoid accumulation.  Lastly, we recommend management to prepare the BRS per savings or current accounts maintained instead of a consolidated BRS to facilitate identification of reconciling items.

We recommend management to require the Municipal Accountant to provide supporting documents on the adjusting entries made for our further evaluation.  Henceforth, we recommend strict adherence with the provisions of PPSAS 27 on the appropriate accounting of Biological Assets by recording the additions, reductions, gains and losses through sale, physical & prices change, and accurately report the carrying values thereof at the year-end.

We recommend that that henceforth, management consolidate its procurement requirements pursuant to COA Circular 92-386 and strictly comply with the rules and regulations on procurement under R.A. 9184.

We recommend management to review and evaluate the performance of waterworks in order to identify factors that cause the continuous incurrence of losses in its operations.  We, likewise, recommend management to strictly follow the provisions stated in the LEE Manual as suggested by DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 Series of 2016 to decide whether to use economic enterpise as the mode of public service and utility provision to its constituency and to assess whether the existing economic enterprise is still viable and shall continue to operate.
We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the report and submit as soon as possible the AAPSI for CY 2017 AAR.  Henceforth, we recommend management strictly comply with the submission of the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the ensuing COA Annual Audit Reports.

Also we recommend that the BAC, in case of failure or inability of the bidder with the SCRB, shall disqualify the bidder and declare the bidding a failure and proceed with the procurement process as provided for in Sections 21 and 25 of the IRR of RA 9184.
 
Henceforth, the BAC members are enjoined to strictly comply with the proper procurement procedures pursuant to the provisions of RA 9184 and it’s IRR to prevent disallowances in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the municipality comply with the existing
regulations for the composition of the LDDRM Office and fill up all the vacant
positions and hire additional staffs as plantilla positions required to perform the
functions of administration and training, research and planning and operations
and warning.

We recommend that the hiring of the job order workers be limited to
emergency or intermittent work and should not, in any case, be made to perform
functions which are part of the job description of the agency’s existing regular
employees.

We also recommend that CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No.1, 2. 2017 be
strictly followed specially the transitory provisions otherwise the sanction
provided therein shall be meted.

We recommend that in the post-qualification process the BAC should
strictly verify, validate and ascertain all statements made and documents
submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid in accordance with the
provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR)
otherwise the non-compliant bidder shall be notified of its disqualification.

We also recommend that RA 9184 and its IRR should be strictly complied
to prevent disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the HoPE shall determine that the bidder Single
Calculated Responsive Bid (SCRB) was not at fault for its failure or inability to
make good its bid by entering into contract with the procuring entity otherwise
the bid security shall be forfeited and imposed the appropriate sanctions
provided in the IRR of RA 9184.

Also we recommend that the BAC, in case of failure or inability of the
bidder with the SCRB, shall disqualify the bidder and declare the bidding a
failure and proceed with the procurement process as provided for in Sections 21
and 25 of the IRR of RA 9184.

Henceforth, the BAC members are enjoined to strictly comply with the
proper procurement procedures pursuant to the provisions of RA 9184 and it’s
IRR to prevent disallowances in audit.

We recommend that the BAC and BAC Secretariat review all requisitions
and see to it that brand names should not be indicated pursuant to Section 18 of
the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure that there is fair competition and not
circumvent the Procurement Law. Failure to do so will be a ground for
disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the management ensure that the required and
appropriate withholding taxes are deducted from payments made in accordance
with the pertinent BIR regulations and the same are remitted to the BIR within
the prescribed period.

We recommend that the Municipality Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to enforce more efficient and effective administration and collection of
real property and special education taxes so as to lessen the tax receivable
account balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local School board limit the utilization of the Special
Education Fund to the programs and projects defined under RA 7160 and DECS,
DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 in order to maximize the benefits that the public
school students and teachers could avail under this fund.

We recommend that the municipality ensure that the 20% development funds be
optimally and promptly utilized to help attain desirable socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes and see to it that the
programs and projects funded out of the funds are in accordance with the
provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum provisions of Joint Circular 1-11
for the programming for development project.

We recommend that Management submit the authority or basis for the hiring of a
Project Engineer and an explanation/justification on the need to hire a Project
Engineer when the agency already has a Municipal Engineer as required in the
Notice of Suspension.

We recommend that Management consider appointing a permanent Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Officer who will run and manage the LDRRMO a
mandatory office, to focus on the main functions of the Office which is the
implementation of the disaster programs of the Municipality in compliance with Rule
1, Section 3 (e) and Rule 5, Section 4 (1) of the IRR of RA 10121.

We recommend that the Municipality comply with the requirements of the
Notice of Suspension for the submission of authority or basis of charging the above
expenses against the LDRRM fund and not against the regular budget of the
LDRRMO and the accomplishment reports of the job-order personnel whose
compensation were charged against the LDRRMF.

We also recommend that the members of the LDRRM Council ensure the
proper and efficient utilization of the LDRRMF by complying the provisions of the
DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and other pertinent laws.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor assign an official or employee to
conduct pre-Inspection of the properties subject to repair and post-Inspection of the
work accomplished and submit the corresponding evaluation or inspection reports
thereof.
